hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: Solver must infer which fstab row merge, unit option sync, and consumer lane surfaces control mount_fingerprint agreement across held-out remount orderings; constraint-complete contract alone does not name broken coupling.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Robust generalization coordinates row_merge_a table synthesis, opt_sync_b unit option reconciliation, and lane_notify_c consumer propagation across three Go package roots plus the public lad_check ladder driver.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Instruction is constraint-complete about remount ladder outcomes without naming fstab/unit/consumer fix route or generalization recipe; agent must design cross-surface mount state invariant.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Unit-only or table-only local fixes pass public tier_alpha/tier_beta smoke while held-out bind-move reorderings, duplicated recovery passes, and repeat migration poison rerun safety.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Requires fstab/systemd mount-unit/propagation coupling beyond generic config editing or textbook atomic-mount recipes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After disclosing lad_check command, ladder_report schema, recovery reset workflow, and mount_fingerprint contract, solver still must trace multi-surface provenance through Go sources and held-out tier orderings.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Public tiers tier_alpha and tier_beta are named; held-out orderings are behavioral combinations, not guessing one corrupt file among hundreds.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Editing one fstab row or replaying one mount slice is insufficient; verifier reruns full ladder with reordered and duplicated recovery.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix must express general remount robustness across ro-rw toggles, bind moves, and propagation changes, not hardcode public tier_alpha/tier_beta outcomes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Honest constraint-complete prompt exposes checked behavior without naming row_merge_a, opt_sync_b, lane_notify_c, or broken remount phase.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Anti-static-output rules are guardrails; difficulty comes from adversarial variant ladder and coordinated mount generalization.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Not a deps/timeout/footer task; core challenge is mount-variant generalization in Go orchestrator sources.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three distinct pkg roots on fix frontier; p8 stub trap and second-pass guard block one-helper patches.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle coordinates go build plus three symbol edits across tab, unit, cons; not a single-file wholesale rewrite.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Solve is not one fstab knob or phase table; requires runtime behavior changes across table merge, unit sync, and consumer notification paths.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns banned from fix-path symbols via code_forbidden_tokens; opaque row_merge_a-style names on frontier.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix symbols row_merge_a, opt_sync_b, lane_notify_c are opaque; decoys stub_* rhyme structurally without matching prompt bullets.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Not safe-extraction, jail hardening, or policy-knob transcription; domain-specific mount-variant generalization.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Mount orchestration semantics, not security theater.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fstab row merge, unit option carry, and consumer lane refresh interact; fixing one layer breaks others without coordinated model.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker/pytest harness adds realism but hardness is in mount coupling and variant ladder, not container count.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Agent must run lad_check ladder, compare mount_fingerprint vs baseline, and experiment across log tails and held-out tier orderings before patching.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Residual-hardness remains after full disclosure; targeted at <=20% worst-model pass rate per Part E.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Four discovery items listed in discovery_budget covering unit/fstab disagreement, consumer stale rows, propagation refresh ordering, and remote option arm divergence.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: adversarial_generalization with constraint-complete framing; public remount contract complete but solution route and broken phase hidden.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three implementation topologies enumerated in topology_enumeration; each requires >=3 coordinated components and no single location passes full verifier.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic lad_check ladder with mount_fingerprint and numeric band assertions; fixed seeds and idempotent reset commands.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Public contract names command, output path, public tiers, and ladder_report schema in pact_m3.md.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Expert with production mount orchestration experience can generalize remount behavior in hours; minimized Go workspace well under 100k LOC.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Requires professional systems-administration debugging; undergrads cannot one-shot without deep codebase engagement and variant experiments.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Mirrors paid work on fstab/systemd mount-unit drift, bind propagation, and remount ladder correctness in production fleets.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Grades regenerated ladder_report.json and cross-run mount fingerprints, not patch process or tool choice.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Instruction states remount ladder contract and public checker command but not which table/unit/consumer surface must generalize or how to reconcile provenance; codebase experiments and held-out tier runs required.

discovery_budget.discovery: Fstab generator emits nofail and remote option strings that systemd unit synthesizer normalizes differently per variant arm, making interim rows look aligned.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the disagreeing surface collapses search to one obvious merge or sync edit.

discovery_budget.discovery: Bind relocation updates mount table rows but consumer registry retains stale lane attachments that pass public tiers until propagation refresh runs.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing consumer lane authority selects notify_c without table/unit coupling search.

discovery_budget.discovery: Read-only to read-write toggle without unit option replay accepts smoke paths unless mount slice bytes and JSON logs agree on mount_fingerprint.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/pkg/tab/merge_a.go row serialization boundary plus environment/fixtures/blk/*.mnt slices
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing ro-rw phase selects merge_a without unit/consumer coordination.

discovery_budget.discovery: Repeat migration removes anchor bytes consumed on the next recovery pass, passing first remount while repeat mig8 poisons rerun safety.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/migrations/mig8.sh documented recovery plus environment/pkg/cons/notify_c.go anchor preservation hooks
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Migration path naming is a forbidden category leak for filesystem_state_reconstruction.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Public prompt states required remount ladder behavior across ro-rw toggles, bind moves, propagation changes, and repeated recovery without naming broken merge phase, unit sync path, or fix symbols.

attack_path.summary: Agent hardcodes public tier_alpha/tier_beta mount_fingerprint filters, passes p8 stub trap but fails held-out tier reorderings and second-pass migration poison tests.
attack_path.reasoning: Fixture-specific blocklist aligns public examples while partial log tails, stale consumer rows, and anchor-destructive migration still diverge on duplicated recovery and bind-move variants.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Coordinate row_merge_a fstab merge, opt_sync_b unit option reconciliation, and lane_notify_c consumer propagation refresh before remount replay.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Still spans three pkg roots and rebuild/checker rerun; not a one-line nofail flag or delete-row shortcut.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Post-disclosure residual work requires tracing multi-surface mount provenance through Go sources and held-out tier experiments, not mapping one public bullet to one obvious function.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After lad_check command, ladder_report schema, and recovery reset workflow are public, solver must infer general remount robustness across reordered bind moves, duplicated passes, and migration-repeat cycles.

topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Merging fstab rows without unit sync leaves divergent option strings; consumer lanes still stale so held-out reorder and second-pass tests fail.
Unit-only fix clears public tiers but partial table tails and anchor-destructive migration break tier_gamma/tier_delta held-out orderings.
Consumer-only refresh passes first recovery but fstab/unit disagreement and partial mount slices still diverge on duplicated pass per test_second_pass_guard.

symbol_table.purpose: Serializes mount slice bytes and table tail into on-disk row sink
Reconciles unit option slots from loaded mount slice view
Refreshes consumer lane attachments while preserving remount anchor set

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Legacy table writer for offline benchmarks without tail merge
No-op option refresh for builds without slot reconciliation
Formats p8 interim rows without preserving anchor bytes

renames_during_drafting.reason: mount_fingerprint is an instruction noun banned from test names
recovery is an instruction noun banned from fix-path symbols
ladders and checker are instruction nouns banned from test names

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: fixtures/q8/p8_stub.json interim rows and partial tier_alpha smoke tallies
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models declare done early when public tiers show healthy interim rows
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Verifier requires terminal mount_fingerprint convergence in ladder_report.json

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: fstab table rows, unit option JSON, and binary mount slices under fixtures/blk
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models fix single file format while breaking cross-format mount_fingerprint agreement
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Invariants tie JSON logs to binary outputs per pact_m3.md

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: destructive migration step migrations/mig8.sh with documented idempotent recovery
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models repeat destructive remount command without anchor preservation
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Idempotent recovery path documented publicly in pact_m3.md

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: sampled traces only under fixtures/logs with authoritative bytes in fixtures/blk
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Models infer mount state from incomplete logs rather than required experiments
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Public contract lists required tier replay experiments and lad_check command

calibration_plan.human_sanity: Systems engineer reproduces failing lad_check locally using bundled log tails and mount slices only
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: Block hardcoded ladder_report.json, blocklist filters matching only public tiers, test edits, stale-doc shortcuts
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Remove false-green interim layer, then cross-format fingerprint layer, then duplicate-pass layer; expect monotonic difficulty drop

pass_rate_target.basis: Part E Hard threshold on worst-model accuracy; verifier-offline via Dockerfile-baked go/pytest with no runtime installs in test.sh under allow_internet=false; post-upload difficulty classification after platform agent runs

metrics.criterion: lad_check ladder exit 0 and mount_fingerprint matches baseline within documented bands
mount_fingerprint cross-recovered-path agreement and duplicate-pass monotonicity
idempotent reset preserves fixture mount slice integrity after mig8.sh
all public and held-out tier rows present in terminal report artifact

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward.txt=1 only when weighted score >= 0.90 with no zero in functional_correctness or hidden_invariants

subtype_milestone_plan.sequential_dependency: Conceptually staged remount variant phases delivered as one adversarial_generalization task
subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: Single-container local verifier; Go module tree and lad_check baked in Dockerfile

platform_files.role: natural constraint-complete public prompt derived from Public contract
verifier entrypoint; invoke pre-installed pytest only
build definition; pre-install pinned go, pytest, lad_check
local authoring artifact mirroring symbol table

task_files.role: report schema, mount_fingerprint rule, band classes, recovery command
destructive remount reset with documented recovery
idempotent scratch seed bytes for mig8 recovery
go build entry for module tree

fix_frontier.distribution: pkg/tab fstab table, pkg/unit mount unit sync, pkg/cons consumer lane roots
fix_frontier.naming_policy: Opaque symbols from construction manifest symbol_table only
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: Decoys may rhyme structurally; oracle must not edit decoy bodies

task_shape.collapse_risk: hardcoded blocklist passes visible fixtures only

category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: Observable symptoms, public commands, verifier contracts for mount variant robustness, remount ladder tiers, and mount_fingerprint schema.
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: Root cause, fix location, patch recipe, oracle transcript, inode/source-of-truth choice, or reconstruction algorithm.
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Three or more coupled subsystems (fstab table, unit synthesizer, consumer registry) must align for remount ladder generalization.
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: False greens from local checks or golden fixtures; one manifest repair shortcuts.
category_profile.coverage_role: Strengthens filesystem_state_reconstruction coverage via fstab variant remount ladder adversarial generalization.

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low — opaque fix-path symbols and neutral path tokens (pact_m3, blk, q8, vault, replay) precommitted
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low — contract uses digests and numeric band fields, not boolean verdict tables
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low — fix frontier spread across tab, unit, cons package roots
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: medium — some invariants live only in cross-artifact mount_fingerprint behavior tied across JSON logs and binary slices

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: lad_check --matrix-full documented in instruction.md with output path /app/output/ladder_report.json
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: yes at product level — produce remount workflow surviving variant ladder without algorithm or patch recipe
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: ladder_report.json path and schema public; static hand-written report insufficient
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: environment/docs/pact_m3.md for mount_fingerprint sha256 and numeric band classes

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: Hardness from coupled mount behavior across table, unit, and consumer surfaces, not harness brittleness

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: No promoted reference in docs/reference_tasks/index.json covers Go adversarial_generalization fstab remount ladder; bind-propagation-orphan-inodes is repair_existing_system Rust and not promoted

realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: Production system-administration pattern where fstab generator and systemd mount units disagree on options, nofail mounts mask ordering bugs, and remote filesystem arms diverge; minimized to local Go mount orchestrator lab
realism_source.minimization_preserves: Causal coupling in fstab variant remount ladder across table merge, unit sync, consumer propagation, and verifier-visible mount_fingerprint identity
